Fit a Causal Forest

Source: R/causalForest.R
causal_forest.Rd

Fit a Causal Forest

Usage

causal_forest(
  X,
  Y,
  W,
  Y.hat = NULL,
  W.hat = NULL,
  num.trees = 2000,
  sample.weights = NULL,
  clusters = NULL,
  equalize.cluster.weights = FALSE,
  sample.fraction = 0.5,
  mtry = min(ceiling(sqrt(ncol(X)) + 20), ncol(X)),
  min.node.size = 5,
  honesty = TRUE,
  honesty.fraction = 0.5,
  honesty.prune.leaves = TRUE,
  alpha = 0.05,
  imbalance.penalty = 0,
  stabilize.splits = TRUE,
  ci.group.size = 2,
  tune.parameters = "none",
  compute.oob.predictions = TRUE,
  num.threads = 1,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

X

Numeric covariate matrix (n x p)

Y

Numeric outcome vector (length n)

W

Binary or continuous treatment vector (length n)

Y.hat

Pre-estimated E[Y|X]; estimated if NULL

W.hat

Pre-estimated E[W|X]; estimated if NULL

num.trees

Number of trees

sample.weights

Observation weights; uniform if NULL

clusters

Cluster IDs for cluster-robust sampling (optional)

equalize.cluster.weights

Balance sampling across clusters

sample.fraction

Fraction of data per tree

mtry

Features tried per split

min.node.size

Minimum observations in a leaf

honesty

Use honest splitting

honesty.fraction

Fraction of subsample used for estimation

honesty.prune.leaves

Remove leaves with no estimation samples

alpha

Minimum split fraction (regularization)

imbalance.penalty

Penalty on unbalanced splits

stabilize.splits

Use W residuals in split criterion

ci.group.size

Trees grouped for variance estimation

tune.parameters

"none" or "all"

compute.oob.predictions

Compute out-of-bag CATE predictions

num.threads

Parallel threads (uses parallel package if > 1)

seed

Random seed for reproducibility

Value

A causal_forest object

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# causal_forest(...)
} # }
